title: Area Converter — Convert m², ft², acres, hectares & more
description: Convert area units instantly: square meters, square feet, acres, hectares, square kilometers, square miles. Free area unit converter.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: How do you convert square meters to square feet?
faq.a1: 1 square meter = 10.7639 square feet. To convert: multiply square meters by 10.7639. To convert square feet to square meters: multiply by 0.0929. This conversion follows directly from the definition of the foot: 1 foot = 0.3048 meters (exact), so 1 square foot = 0.3048² = 0.09290304 m² exactly. Example: a 50 m² apartment = 50 × 10.7639 = 538.2 square feet. A 1,200 sq ft house = 1,200 × 0.0929 = 111.5 m².
faq.q2: What is the difference between an acre and a hectare?
faq.a2: An acre = 43,560 square feet = 4,046.86 m². A hectare = 10,000 m² (exactly 100m × 100m). 1 hectare = 2.47105 acres. The acre is used in the U.S., UK, and some Commonwealth countries for land measurement. The hectare is the global standard used in agriculture, forestry, and real estate in most of the world. An acre is approximately the size of an American football field (without end zones). A hectare is slightly less than the area of an international soccer pitch (typically 105m × 68m = 7,140 m²).
faq.q3: How many square feet are in an acre?
faq.a3: Exactly 43,560 square feet per acre. This specific number has a historical origin: 1 acre was originally defined as the area a yoke of oxen could plow in one day — approximately a strip of land 1 furlong (660 feet) long and 1 chain (66 feet) wide. 660 × 66 = 43,560. The furlong (from 'furrow-long') and chain (the surveyor's chain of 100 links) were standard agricultural measurement tools in medieval England. Despite the archaic origin, the acre remains the standard U.S. unit for land parcels, farmland, and property measurements.
faq.q4: What does square kilometer represent and how does it compare to square miles?
faq.a4: 1 square kilometer (km²) = 1,000,000 m² = 0.386102 square miles. 1 square mile = 2.58999 km². Square kilometers are used globally for measuring city areas, country sizes, and geographic regions. Square miles are used primarily in the U.S. and UK for geographic areas. New York City covers approximately 784 km² (302 sq miles). London covers approximately 1,572 km² (607 sq miles). Russia, the world's largest country, covers approximately 17.1 million km² (6.6 million sq miles).
faq.q5: How do I convert acres to hectares?
faq.a5: 1 acre = 0.404686 hectares. To convert acres to hectares, multiply by 0.404686. To convert hectares to acres, multiply by 2.47105. Example: a 250-acre farm = 250 × 0.404686 = 101.17 hectares. A 500-hectare forest = 500 × 2.47105 = 1,235.5 acres. Agricultural production data from the USDA uses acres for U.S. crops, while the UN FAO (Food and Agriculture Organization) uses hectares globally — making this conversion essential for international agricultural comparisons.
faq.q6: What units of area are used in real estate internationally?
faq.a6: Real estate measurement varies significantly by country. U.S. and Canada: square feet (interior living space), acres (land). UK: square feet (interior), acres (land), with metric increasingly required in planning. Europe (EU): square meters (interior), hectares (land). India: square feet and square meters for apartments; cents (1/100 acre), grounds, and guntas in southern states; bigha and katha in northern and eastern states. China: square meters (m²) and mu (1 mu ≈ 666.7 m² ≈ 0.0667 hectare). Japan: tsubo (1 tsubo ≈ 3.3 m²), used for room sizes in older listings. International property portals like Rightmove and Zillow typically convert all listings to local preferred units.
faq.q7: How accurate are the conversion factors used in this tool?
faq.a7: All metric-to-metric conversions (m², km², hectares) are exact by definition. Conversions involving the acre and square foot use the internationally defined foot (0.3048 meters exactly), making: 1 sq ft = 0.09290304 m² exactly, 1 acre = 4,046.8564224 m² exactly, 1 hectare = 2.4710538... acres (rational but non-terminating). For all practical real estate and agricultural applications, these conversions are accurate to at least 6 significant figures — far beyond the precision of any physical measurement.
faq.q8: Can this converter handle national or continental areas?
faq.a8: Yes. The converter accepts values up to 10¹⁵ square meters (one petameter²), which is sufficient for any land area on Earth or beyond. For reference: Earth's total land area is approximately 1.49 × 10¹⁴ m² (149 million km²). The United States covers 9.83 × 10¹² m² (9.83 million km²). At continental scales, the display switches to scientific notation for readability, and the relevant comparison units are square kilometers or square miles rather than acres or square feet.

content.whatIs:
An area converter translates measurements of two-dimensional surface area between the full range of units used globally — from square millimeters for tiny surfaces to square kilometers and square miles for geographic regions, including agricultural units (acres, hectares) and the full range of metric SI prefixed units. Enter a value in any unit and instantly see the equivalent in every other unit, making cross-system area comparisons simple and precise.

Area measurement is at the heart of real estate, agriculture, urban planning, architecture, and geographic analysis. The challenge is that different industries, countries, and historical traditions have established incompatible unit systems. A U.S. farmland sale is described in acres; the same land parcel in a UN agricultural report becomes hectares; an international investor reading about the property might think in square meters or square kilometers. A floor plan in the United States gives square footage; the same apartment listed in Germany or France gives square meters. This constant cross-system translation is a practical daily need in any field that deals with area measurement internationally.

The divergence between the U.S. customary system and the metric system is particularly stark for area: the acre has no metric equivalent and derives from a medieval agricultural concept (the amount of land a yoke of oxen could plow in one day), while the hectare is a clean metric unit equal to exactly 10,000 square meters. Neither maps neatly onto the other, making precise calculation essential rather than approximation. This converter provides exact conversion factors derived from the internationally defined foot (0.3048 meters) for all imperial units, ensuring results accurate to any practical precision requirement.
content.formula:
SI Metric area units:
1 km² = 1,000,000 m²
1 m² = 10,000 cm² = 1,000,000 mm²
1 hectare = 10,000 m² (100m × 100m)

Imperial / U.S. customary:
1 square mile = 640 acres = 27,878,400 sq ft
1 acre = 43,560 sq ft = 4,840 sq yd
1 square yard = 9 sq ft
1 square foot = 144 sq in
1 sq ft = 0.09290304 m² (exact, from 1 ft = 0.3048 m)

Key cross-system conversions:
1 m² = 10.7639 sq ft = 1.19599 sq yd
1 km² = 0.386102 sq miles = 247.105 acres
1 acre = 4,046.86 m² = 0.404686 hectares
1 hectare = 10,000 m² = 2.47105 acres
1 sq mile = 2.58999 km² = 259 hectares
content.example:
Example 1 — Real estate: apartment in Europe vs. U.S.
An 85 m² apartment listed in Paris.
85 × 10.7639 = 914.9 sq ft
Equivalent: a 915 sq ft apartment in New York listings.

Example 2 — Agricultural land sale:
340 acres farmland in Iowa.
340 × 0.404686 = 137.6 hectares
340 × 4,046.86 = 1,375,932 m²
340 × 0.53125 sq miles ≈ 0.531 sq miles

Example 3 — Urban planning:
City park planned at 12 hectares.
12 × 2.47105 = 29.65 acres
12 × 10,000 = 120,000 m²
12 × 0.01 km² = 0.12 km²

Example 4 — Country comparison:
Belgium: 30,528 km² | Netherlands: 41,543 km²
30,528 km² = 11,782 sq miles = 7,540,544 acres = 3,052,800 hectares
content.howItWorks_1: Enter the numerical area value in the 'Value' field. The value must be non-negative (area cannot be negative). Decimal values are supported for precision conversions. Very large values (national or continental scales) are accepted up to 10¹⁵.
content.howItWorks_2: Select the source unit from the 'From' dropdown. The converter supports square meters, square feet, square kilometers, square miles, acres, hectares, square centimeters, square millimeters, and square yards. All conversions compute simultaneously.
content.howItWorks_3: Results appear instantly in all supported units. For real estate comparisons, focus on sq ft vs. m² for interior space and acres vs. hectares for land area. For geographic comparisons, use km² or sq miles. All outputs use the same precision to allow direct comparison.
content.whyItMatters:
Area conversion mistakes in real estate have cost buyers and sellers significant amounts of money. The most common confusion is between square meters and square feet — a factor of approximately 10.76 that, when missed, can make a 90 m² apartment appear to be approximately 90 square feet (a tiny room) or a 1,000 square foot house appear to be 1,000 m² (a palatial estate). International property portals that display listings from multiple countries must convert all listings to the buyer's local unit — errors in this conversion have been documented in cross-border property transactions.

In agriculture, the acre-to-hectare conversion is the divide between U.S. and international commodity markets. USDA crop production reports use acres; FAO statistics use hectares; farm equipment specifications may use either. A farmer purchasing equipment rated for a specific per-hectare application rate must correctly convert their acreage before calibrating — incorrect conversion affects fertilizer and pesticide application quantities directly, with crop yield and environmental compliance consequences.

For urban planners, architects, and construction managers, area is the fundamental unit of cost estimation. Building cost is typically quoted per square meter or per square foot — converting between these directly converts cost estimates. A contractor quoting $150 per square foot for construction in the U.S. would quote approximately $1,615 per square meter in metric terms. Project managers working across systems need these conversions accurate to avoid budget errors.
content.limitations:
This converter handles the standard area units used in real estate, agriculture, and geography. It does not include some specialized or historical units that may appear in older legal documents or specific regional contexts: the rood (1/4 acre = 1,011.7 m²), the perch or square rod (1/160 acre = 25.29 m²), or the township (36 square miles = 23,310 hectares) used in U.S. public land survey descriptions. Property deeds in the U.S. recorded before the 20th century may use chains, rods, or links that require specialized survey unit converters.

The converter also does not handle some Asian regional units: the Japanese tsubo (≈3.306 m²) and tsubo-based units like the ken, cho, and tan; the Chinese mu (≈666.7 m²) and its subdivisions; the Indian bigha (which varies from state to state — 0.165 hectares in Punjab to 0.67 hectares in West Bengal, making direct comparison impossible without knowing the regional variant). For these units, always verify the specific local definition before converting.

For geographic comparisons, note that 'area' of countries and water bodies is measured at the surface and does not account for topographic relief. A mountainous country measured as 83,879 km² (Austria, for example) would have a larger actual surface area if measured along the terrain rather than projected on a flat plane. This projection effect is relevant for precision land surveys but not for the comparisons this tool supports.
content.tips_1: For quick mental conversion between sq ft and m², use the approximation: divide sq ft by 10.76 (or multiply by 0.093) to get m². For reverse, multiply m² by 10.76. A 1,500 sq ft home ≈ 1,500/10.76 ≈ 139 m². A 120 m² apartment ≈ 120 × 10.76 ≈ 1,291 sq ft. This approximation is accurate within 0.05% of the exact value.
content.tips_2: When comparing international property listings, always confirm which unit system the listing uses — and never assume. European listings universally use m²; U.S. listings use sq ft; UK listings use both but often present sq ft for interior space and acres for land. Some UK and Australian listings use sq m. Misidentifying the unit system can create apparent price differences of 10× in cost-per-area calculations.
content.tips_3: To estimate acreage from a property's street dimensions in the U.S.: divide the total square footage of the lot by 43,560. A lot measuring 100 ft × 200 ft = 20,000 sq ft / 43,560 = 0.459 acres. Standard U.S. residential lots in suburban areas typically range from 0.1 to 0.5 acres (4,356 to 21,780 sq ft).
content.tips_4: For agricultural yield comparisons, note that yield per unit area is a compound unit: 'bushels per acre' (U.S.) vs. 'tonnes per hectare' (international). Converting these requires knowing both the area unit conversion and the mass unit conversion. The conversion factor for yield: multiply 'bushels/acre' × 0.0673 × (commodity weight factor) to get 'tonnes/hectare'. For example, corn yield of 180 bushels/acre × 0.0628 ≈ 11.3 t/ha (using corn at 56 lbs/bushel).
